1.template: The voice of one crying in the wilderness, {0} ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.

1.title: The prophet of the wilderness
1.text: John the Baptist wore camel’s hair and a leather girdle, and ate locusts and wild honey — marks of a prophet who lived apart from the world, like Elijah.

1.template: I indeed have baptized you with water: but he shall baptize you with the Holy {0}.

1.question: What did the voice from heaven say when Jesus was baptized?
options: “Thou art my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased”
“Prepare ye the way of the Lord”
“This is the king of the Jews”

1.template: Come ye after me, and I will make you to become {0} of men

1.question: What did James and John do when Jesus called them?
options: They left their father Zebedee in the ship and followed him
They asked to think it over
They went to tell the priests

1.template: And there was in their {0} a man with an unclean spirit; and he cried out,
And immediately his fame spread abroad throughout all the region round about {0}.
And all the city was {0} together at the door.
And he said unto them, Let us go into the next towns, that I may preach there also: for {0} came I forth.
And he straitly charged him, and {0} sent him away;

2.template: And they come unto him, {0} one sick of the palsy, which was borne of four.

2.title: Through the roof
2.text: Four men carried a paralytic to Jesus, but unable to get through the crowd, they uncovered the roof and lowered him on his bed. Jesus commended their faith.

2.template: And immediately when {0} perceived in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, he said unto them, Why reason ye these things in your hearts?
And he went forth again by the sea side; and all the {0} resorted unto him, and he taught them.

2.question: Why did Jesus say he ate with publicans and sinners?
options: Because he came to call sinners to repentance, not the righteous
Because there was no one else

2.template: And it came to pass, that he went through the corn fields on the sabbath day; and his {0} began, as they went, to pluck the ears of corn.
The {0} was made for man, and not man for the sabbath

3.question: What did the unclean spirits proclaim when they saw Jesus?
options: That he was the Son of God
That he was the king of the Jews
That he was John the Baptist

3.title: Sons of thunder
3.text: Jesus gave James and John the Aramaic nickname Boanerges, 'sons of thunder,' perhaps for their fiery temperament.

3.question: How did Jesus refute the charge that he cast out devils by Beelzebub?
options: By denying he cast out devils
By saying a kingdom divided against itself cannot stand
By asking the scribes for a sign

3.question: Which sin, according to Jesus, shall never be forgiven?

3.template: whosoever shall do the {0} of God, the same is my brother

4.question: Where fell the seed that the fowls of the air devoured?

4.template: because it had no {0}, it withered away

4.question: Why did Jesus speak to the multitude in parables, as he told the disciples?
options: So that no one would ever understand
Because to them it was given to know the mystery of the kingdom, but not to those without
Because he could not explain otherwise

4.question: Jesus said, 'With what measure ye mete...' — what?
options: it shall be measured to you

4.question: In the parable, how does the seed grow after the man casts it into the ground?
options: Only if he waters it daily
Of itself the earth brings forth fruit, he knows not how
Only with the help of angels

4.template: It is like a grain of {0} seed, which, when it is sown

4.title: The smallest of seeds
4.text: The mustard seed was proverbially tiny, yet the plant grew into a shrub where birds could nest — an image familiar to Galilean farmers.

4.question: How did Jesus teach the multitude, and what did he do privately with the disciples?
options: He spoke only secretly to all
He spoke in parables and explained all to the disciples privately

4.question: What did the disciples say when Jesus stilled the storm?
options: What manner of man is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?

5.question: Where did the man with the unclean spirit, whom no one could bind, dwell?

5.template: saying, My name is {0}: for we are many

5.question: Where did the unclean spirits go, and what happened?
options: They entered the swine, which drowned in the sea

5.template: began to publish in {0} how great things Jesus had done for him

5.question: Who was Jairus, who begged Jesus for his daughter?
options: One of the rulers of the synagogue

5.template: which had an issue of blood {0} years

5.question: What did Jesus say to the woman healed of the issue of blood?
options: Daughter, thy faith hath made thee whole; go in peace

5.question: What did Jesus say about Jairus' daughter, whom they said was dead?
options: The damsel is not dead, but sleepeth

5.title: The Aramaic words
5.text: Mark preserves Jesus' exact words in Aramaic — 'Talitha cumi' — and translates them, a hallmark of his Gospel.

6.question: Why could Jesus do no mighty work in his own country?
How did Jesus send the twelve to preach?
Who did king Herod think Jesus was?
options: John the Baptist risen from the dead

6.template: knowing that he was a {0} man and an holy

6.question: What did the apostles do when they gathered to Jesus?
options: They asked to rest at home
They told him all they had done and taught
They asked for places of honor

6.question: Why did the disciples want to send the multitude away?
options: The place was desert and it was late, and they had nothing to eat

6.title: Two hundred pennyworth
6.text: A denarius was about a day's wage; the two hundred pennyworth the disciples mention came to nearly eight months' pay.

6.question: With how many loaves and fishes did Jesus feed the five thousand?
options: Seven loaves and a few fishes

6.template: Be of good cheer: it is I; be not {0}.

6.question: What did the sick ask in order to be healed as Jesus passed?
options: That he pray in the temple
To touch at least the border of his garment
That he take them to Jerusalem

7.question: What tradition of the elders did the Pharisees keep before eating?

7.template: ye reject the {0} of God, that ye may keep your own tradition

7.question: What did 'Corban' mean, used to avoid helping one's parents?
According to Jesus, what does NOT defile a man?
options: What enters from outside, for it goes to the belly, not the heart

7.question: Where, according to Jesus, do the evils that defile a man come from?
options: From within, out of the heart of men
From the tradition of the elders

7.title: Faith beyond Israel
7.text: Tyre and Sidon were Gentile Phoenician cities; the Syrophenician woman was not a Jew, yet her faith won healing for her daughter — a sign the gospel would reach beyond Israel.

7.template: to take the children’s {0}, and to cast it unto the dogs

7.question: What miracle did Jesus do for the deaf man, saying 'Ephphatha'?
options: Opened his ears and loosed his tongue

pairs.right: Greek woman whose daughter was healed

8.question: How many loaves did the disciples have for the four thousand?

8.template: of the broken meat that was left {0} baskets

8.question: Against what 'leaven' did Jesus warn the disciples?
options: The leaven of the Pharisees and of Herod

8.title: A two-stage healing
8.text: Only Mark records this gradual healing: the blind man first sees men 'as trees' and then clearly — unique among Jesus' miracles.

8.question: What did Peter answer when Jesus asked who he was?

8.template: deny himself, and take up his {0}, and follow me

8.question: What did Jesus say about whoever is ashamed of him and his words?
options: The Son of man shall be ashamed of him when he comes in glory
He shall only lose his reward
He shall not enter the synagogue

9.question: Who appeared with Jesus on the mount of transfiguration?

9.template: This is my {0} Son: hear him

9.question: The disciples asked why the scribes said Elias must come first. What did Jesus answer?
options: That Elias was indeed come, and they did to him as they wished
That Elias would come only at the end of the world

9.question: Why did the father bring his son to Jesus rather than leave him with the disciples?
options: Because the disciples could not cast out the dumb spirit

9.question: What did Jesus call that generation which had no faith?
What did the boy's father cry out when Jesus said all things are possible to him that believes?
options: Lord, I believe; help thou mine unbelief
I have no faith at all
Heal him though I believe not

9.question: What did Jesus foretell to the disciples as they passed through Galilee?
options: That the Son of man would be killed and rise the third day
That he would soon reign in Jerusalem
That the temple would be rebuilt
That he would return to Capernaum

9.question: What did Jesus say to whoever wants to be first?
options: He shall be last of all and servant of all
He shall be exalted above others
He must keep the law more than all

pairs.left: He that is not against us
A cup of water in my name

pairs.right: Better a millstone about his neck

9.title: Gehenna
9.text: The word translated 'hell' is Gehenna, the Valley of Hinnom outside Jerusalem, a smouldering refuse dump Jesus used as an image of final ruin.

9.template: Have {0} in yourselves, and have peace one with another

10.question: When the Pharisees asked about divorce, to what did Jesus point them?
options: To what Moses commanded, written for the hardness of heart
To a law of the Romans
To a custom of the Gentiles
To a tradition of the elders

10.question: According to Jesus, how did God make people from the beginning of creation?

10.template: And they twain shall be one {0}

10.question: What did Jesus answer the man who called him 'Good Master'?
options: There is none good but one, that is, God

10.question: What did Jesus say was hard regarding riches and the kingdom of God?
options: It is hard for them that trust in riches to enter the kingdom of God
The rich can never be saved
The poor do not enter the kingdom
One must give half of one's goods

10.template: It is easier for a {0} to go through the eye of a needle

10.question: What did Jesus say would happen to him when he went up to Jerusalem?
options: He would be condemned, mocked, killed, and rise the third day
He would be acclaimed by the priests
He would build a new temple

10.question: What did James and John ask of Jesus?
options: To sit at his right and left hand in glory

pairs.right: Came to serve and give his life a ransom

10.question: How did blind Bartimaeus call Jesus when he asked for mercy?

10.title: Bartimaeus, son of Timaeus
10.text: Mark is the only Gospel that names the blind man, Bartimaeus; by calling Jesus 'Son of David' he acknowledges him as the promised Messiah.

11.template: a colt tied, whereon never {0} sat
Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the {0}

11.question: Besides 'Hosanna', what did the crowd acclaim at Jesus' entry?
options: Blessed be the kingdom of our father David
Glory to the temple of Jerusalem
Long live the king of the Romans

11.title: Hosanna
11.text: 'Hosanna' comes from the Hebrew of Psalm 118 and means 'save now!' — a cry of welcome to the king coming in the Lord's name.

11.question: What did Jesus do when he entered the temple in Jerusalem?
options: Drove out those who sold and overthrew the moneychangers' tables
Read the law to the priests

11.question: What did Jesus teach his house should be, and what had they made it?
options: A house of prayer, but they made it a den of thieves
A house of sacrifice, but they made it a market
A house of feasting, but they made it desolate
A house of kings, but they made it poor

pairs.right: The mountain will be cast into the sea
Forgive that ye may be forgiven

11.question: How did Jesus answer the priests who asked by what authority he acted?
options: He asked them whether John's baptism was from heaven or of men
He said his authority came from Caesar
He showed them a sign from heaven
He refused to enter the temple

12.question: In the parable, what would the lord of the vineyard do to the wicked husbandmen?
options: Come, destroy the husbandmen, and give the vineyard to others

12.question: What did Jesus say about the tribute to Caesar?
options: Render to Caesar what is Caesar's, and to God what is God's

12.question: On what subject did the Sadducees try to confound Jesus with the story of seven brothers?
Why, according to Jesus, did the Sadducees err?
options: Because they knew not the scriptures nor the power of God
Because they paid tribute to Caesar
Because they kept not the sabbath

12.question: How did God identify himself in the bush, showing that the dead rise?
options: The God of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob
The God of the living and the dead

12.title: The Shema
12.text: 'Hear, O Israel: the Lord our God is one Lord' is the Shema, the central confession of Jewish faith, recited daily.

12.template: Thou shalt love thy {0} as thyself

12.question: What did Jesus say about the poor widow's offering?
options: She cast in more than all, for she gave all she had
She cast in far too little
She should have given to the temple

13.question: What did Jesus warn the disciples about at the start of the discourse on the end?
options: That many would come in his name saying 'I am Christ' and deceive many
That the temple would be enlarged

13.question: What did Jesus say must first happen?
options: That the gospel be preached among all nations

13.question: What should those in Judea do when they saw the abomination of desolation?
What would the false christs and false prophets do?
options: Signs and wonders to deceive even the elect

pairs.right: Shall come in the clouds with power and glory

13.template: but my {0} shall not pass away

13.title: Not even the Son knows the day
13.text: Jesus teaches that no one knows the day or hour — not the angels, nor the Son, but the Father; the point is to watch, not to set dates.

14.question: What did the woman in Bethany pour on Jesus' head?
options: An ointment of pure spikenard, very precious

14.title: Costly spikenard
14.text: Spikenard was a very costly perfume imported from India; it was worth about three hundred pence, nearly a year's wages.

14.template: ye have the {0} with you always

14.question: What did the disciples go to prepare in the city?
options: The passover, in a large upper room

14.question: What did Jesus say at supper about one of the twelve?
options: That one of them, who ate with him, would betray him
That one of them would be greatest
That all would forsake him forever
That one of them would be healed

14.question: What did Jesus foretell Peter would do before the cock crowed twice?
What did Jesus say to the three disciples in Gethsemane?
options: That his soul was sorrowful unto death; to stay and watch

14.template: not what I {0}, but what thou wilt

pairs.right: Kissed Jesus as a sign to the guards
Cut off the ear of the high priest's servant

14.question: What did the young man following Jesus do when they tried to seize him?
options: Fled naked, leaving the linen cloth

14.question: What false witness did they bring against Jesus before the high priest?
options: That he said he would destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days

14.question: What did Jesus answer when the high priest asked if he was the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?
options: I am; and ye shall see the Son of man at the right hand of power
I am not what you say
Thou hast said it, not I

14.question: What did Peter do when a maid said he had been with Jesus?
options: He denied, saying he knew him not

14.question: What did Peter do when he remembered Jesus' word, after denying him three times?

15.question: What did Pilate ask Jesus, and how did he answer?
options: If he was the King of the Jews; Jesus said: Thou sayest it
If he was a prophet; Jesus denied it
If he paid tribute; Jesus was silent
If he was Elias; Jesus agreed

15.template: And there was one named {0}, which lay bound

15.question: What did the crowd demand, leading Pilate to release Barabbas?
options: That Jesus be scourged and released
That Jesus be taken to Herod

15.question: How did the soldiers mock Jesus?
options: They clothed him in purple and put a crown of thorns on him
They bound him to a single pillar

15.title: Simon of Cyrene
15.text: Mark identifies Simon as 'the father of Alexander and Rufus,' names likely known to the early Roman Christians for whom he wrote.

15.template: Golgotha, which is, being interpreted, The place of a {0}.

pairs.right: The prisoner released instead of Jesus

15.question: What did Jesus cry out with a loud voice at the ninth hour, on the cross?
options: My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?
Father, into thy hands I commend my spirit

15.question: What happened in the temple at the moment Jesus died?
options: The veil was torn in two, from top to bottom

15.question: What did the centurion say when he saw how Jesus died?
options: Truly this man was the Son of God
I am innocent of this blood

15.question: Who asked Pilate for Jesus' body and buried it?

16.question: What did the women find when they came to the tomb?
options: The stone rolled away and a young man clothed in white

16.title: The first witness
16.text: Mary Magdalene, out of whom Jesus had cast seven devils, was the first to see him risen and to announce it to the disciples.

pairs.right: Was the first to see the risen Jesus
Were told to preach to every creature
